skill.name: site-architecture
skill.description: Plan website information architecture, including page hierarchy, navigation, URL patterns, visual sitemaps, and internal linking.
skill.summary: Creates practical website architecture plans with page trees, URL maps, navigation specs, and internal linking guidance.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown examples, Mermaid diagrams, URL patterns, and evaluation text rather than executable code. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, active network calls, or command execution was found. The only residual concern is a narrow instruction to read local product marketing context files when present.

low_findings.title: Limited Local Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to read .agents/product-marketing-context.md or .claude/product-marketing-context.md if present. This is relevant to the task and limited to named context files, but it is still local filesystem access that users should understand.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly instruct a conditional local file read. Confidence is high because the behavior is clear, although the scope is narrow and task-relevant.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged many fenced Markdown blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Review found Mermaid diagrams, ASCII navigation examples, URL maps, HTML snippets, and JSON-LD examples, not executable command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced documentation examples and diagrams. No subprocess, shell, eval, or runtime code path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Documentation Noise
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in evaluation prompts, URL examples, page labels, and diagram nodes. Review found no cryptographic API, hash function, encryption routine, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed location is static prose or a site architecture example. There is no code that uses weak algorithms or handles cryptographic material.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Educational Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are schema.org and example.com URLs inside breadcrumb markup examples. They are not used by fetch, curl, browser automation, telemetry, or any other network-capable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only hardcoded URLs reviewed are inside HTML and JSON-LD documentation examples. No active network request mechanism exists in the skill files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is Site Planning Text
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance alerts matched words used for website planning, such as documentation, API, navigation, and site sections. Review found no commands or instructions to enumerate host, network, account, or process information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is about website information architecture and visual sitemap output. No host or network discovery behavior is described.

content.value_statement: Website teams often struggle to organize pages, navigation, URLs, and internal links into a clear structure. This skill turns business context and page inventory into practical architecture plans, sitemaps, and navigation specifications.
actual_capabilities: Creates page hierarchy plans for SaaS, content, e-commerce, documentation, hybrid, and local business sites.
Produces ASCII page trees with recommended URLs for each important page.
Generates Mermaid visual sitemaps for design and planning discussions.
Defines header, footer, sidebar, breadcrumb, and mobile navigation patterns.
Builds URL map tables with parent pages, navigation locations, and priorities.
Recommends internal linking plans, hub pages, spoke pages, and orphan page checks.
limitations: Does not generate or repair XML sitemaps for technical SEO crawlers.
Does not run live crawl audits, analytics reports, or Search Console checks.
Needs accurate business context and page inventory for complete recommendations.
Provides planning guidance, not production implementation in a CMS or router.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Marketing Site
use_cases.description: Turn goals, audiences, and planned content into a clear hierarchy, URL structure, and navigation plan.

use_cases.title: Restructure a Growing Website
use_cases.description: Group scattered pages, reduce navigation clutter, plan redirects, and improve findability for important content.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Handoff Materials
use_cases.description: Create ASCII trees, Mermaid sitemaps, and navigation specs that support wireframes and stakeholder review.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Site Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new website structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan the site architecture for a new website. Ask for any missing business context, then propose the main pages, URL patterns, and navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Visual Sitemap
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a shareable sitemap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a visual sitemap for our website. Include the page hierarchy, major navigation zones, and a short explanation for design stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Restructure Existing Navigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for an overloaded site
prompt_templates.prompt: Our site has too many pages and users cannot find content. Review this page list, group pages by purpose, and recommend a cleaner hierarchy and navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Scaled SEO Sections
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with many programmatic pages
prompt_templates.prompt: We are adding integration, comparison, and template pages at scale. Design the hubs, URL patterns, navigation placement, and internal linking model.

output_examples.input: A SaaS company needs pages for features, pricing, integrations, comparisons, blog, docs, and support.
output: A grouped page hierarchy with homepage, product, resources, docs, company, and legal sections.
A URL map that uses short, readable paths for feature, integration, comparison, and blog pages.
A navigation spec with primary header items, footer columns, breadcrumbs, and internal link priorities.

output_examples.input: A content site has many uncategorized articles and weak internal linking.
output: A hub-and-spoke architecture that groups articles under topic hubs and category pages.
A sidebar and footer navigation plan that exposes key categories without crowding the header.
An internal linking checklist for related articles, pillar pages, and orphan content.

output_examples.input: A redesign team needs a sitemap that designers and stakeholders can review.
output: A concise visual sitemap using Mermaid-style relationships and navigation zones.
A supporting page table that lists each page, parent, URL, navigation location, and priority.

best_practices: Provide business goals, audience segments, page inventory, and existing URL constraints before asking for a full plan.
Use the output as a planning artifact, then review it with design, SEO, content, and engineering stakeholders.
Keep important pages within a few clicks from the homepage and use consistent URL patterns across sections.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for XML sitemap repair, crawl errors, or technical SEO diagnostics.
Do not ask for a final architecture without sharing the page inventory or planned content sections.
Do not approve URL changes without a redirect plan for existing pages that already receive traffic or links.

faq.question: Can this skill create an XML sitemap?
faq.answer: No. It plans human-facing site structure, navigation, URLs, and internal links. Technical XML sitemap work belongs in an SEO audit workflow.

faq.question: Can it help with an existing messy website?
faq.answer: Yes. Share the current page list, known problems, important URLs, and business goals so it can propose a cleaner structure.

faq.question: Does it produce visual sitemaps?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create Mermaid visual sitemaps and ASCII page trees for planning and design review.

faq.question: What site types does it support?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for SaaS, content, e-commerce, documentation, hybrid SaaS plus content, and small business websites.

faq.question: Does it handle internal linking?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends hub pages, spoke pages, contextual links, breadcrumbs, and checks for orphan pages.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the site type, audience, business goals, current or planned pages, top priority pages, and any URLs that must be preserved.
